title: Ensure mounting of cramfs filesystems is disabled
description: The cramfs filesystem type is a compressed read-only Linux filesystem embedded in small footprint systems. A null value for cramfs may point to an exploitable kernel module.
rationale: Removing support for unneeded filesystem types reduces the local attack surface of the server.

title: Ensure filesystem integrity is regularly checked
description: Periodic checking of the filesystem integrity is needed to detect changes to the filesystem.
rationale: Periodic file checking allows the system administrator to determine on a regular basis if critical files have been changed in an unauthorized fashion.
remediation_command: apt install aide aide-common -y && aideinit && cp /var/lib/aide/aide.db.new /var/lib/aide/aide.db && echo '0 5 * * * root /usr/bin/aide.wrapper --config /etc/aide/aide.conf --check' >> /etc/crontab

title: Ensure X Window System is not installed
description: The X Window System provides a Graphical User Interface (GUI) where users can have multiple windows for applications running on the server.
rationale: Unless your organization specifically requires graphical login access via the X Window System, remove it to reduce the potential attack surface.

title: Ensure IP forwarding is disabled
description: The net.ipv4.ip_forward flag is used to tell the system whether it can forward packets or not.
rationale: Setting the flag to 0 ensures that a system with multiple interfaces cannot forward packets.

title: Ensure auditd is installed
description: auditd is the userspace component to the Linux Auditing System. It's responsible for writing audit records to the disk.
rationale: The capturing of system events provides system administrators with information to allow them to determine if unauthorized access to their system is occurring.

title: Ensure permissions on /etc/ssh/sshd_config are configured
description: The /etc/ssh/sshd_config file contains configuration specifications for sshd. The command below sets the owner and group of the file to root.
rationale: The /etc/ssh/sshd_config file needs to be protected from unauthorized changes by non-privileged users.

title: Ensure SSH Protocol is set to 2
description: SSH v1 has significant known vulnerabilities and should not be used.
rationale: SSH v2 provides more secure encryption, authentication, and integrity checking compared to SSH v1.

title: Ensure password creation requirements are configured
description: The pam_pwquality module provides strength checking for new passwords.
rationale: Strong passwords protect systems from being hacked through brute force methods.
remediation_command: apt install libpam-pwquality -y && sed -i '/pam_pwquality.so/c\password requisite pam_pwquality.so retry=3 minlen=14 dcredit=-1 ucredit=-1 ocredit=-1 lcredit=-1' /etc/pam.d/common-password

title: Ensure a separate partition for containers has been created
description: All Docker containers and their data should be stored in a dedicated partition.
rationale: Docker depends on /var/lib/docker as the default directory to store all Docker related files. This can grow rapidly and exhaust disk space causing system issues.
audit_command: mountpoint -- "$(docker info -f '{{ .DockerRootDir }}')" 2>/dev/null && echo 'OK' || echo 'Not a mountpoint'
remediation_command: Create a separate partition and mount it at /var/lib/docker before starting Docker service.

title: Run containers as non-root users
description: Containers should not run as the root user to limit the blast radius of a container breakout.
rationale: Running as root inside a container increases the risk that a container escape will lead to full host compromise.
audit_command: docker ps --quiet | xargs -I{} docker inspect {} --format '{{ .Id }}: User={{ .Config.User }}'
remediation_command:
Add USER directive to Dockerfile: USER nobody:nobody
Or run with: docker run --user 1000:1000 image

title: Ensure Content Trust for Docker is Enabled
description: Content trust is disabled by default in Docker. Content trust provides the ability to use digital signatures for data sent to and received from remote Docker registries.
rationale: Content trust provides the ability to verify the integrity and publisher of all data received from a registry over any channel.

title: Ensure a user for the container has been created
description: Create a non-root user for the container in the Dockerfile for the container image.
rationale: It is a good practice to run the container as a non-root user, if possible. Though user namespace mapping is now available, if a user is already defined in the container image, the container is run as that user by default.
remediation_command:
Add to Dockerfile:
RUN groupadd -r appgroup && useradd -r -g appgroup appuser
USER appuser

title: Ensure that the API server pod specification file permissions are set to 600 or more restrictive
description: Ensure that the API server pod specification file has permissions of 600 or more restrictive.
rationale: The API server pod specification file controls various parameters that set the behavior of the API server. If an attacker can modify the file, they can compromise the cluster.

title: Ensure that the --anonymous-auth argument is set to false
description: Disable anonymous requests to the API server.
rationale: When anonymous requests are enabled, requests that are not rejected by other configured authentication methods are treated as anonymous requests.
remediation_command:
Edit the API server pod specification at /etc/kubernetes/manifests/kube-apiserver.yaml and set:
--anonymous-auth=false

title: Ensure that the --anonymous-auth argument is set to false for kubelet
description: Disable anonymous requests to the Kubelet server.
rationale: When enabled, requests that are not rejected by other configured authentication methods are treated as anonymous requests.
remediation_command:
Edit /etc/kubernetes/kubelet.conf and add:
authentication:
  anonymous:
    enabled: false

title: Ensure the latest Apache HTTP Server software is installed
description: The most recent Apache HTTP Server software should be installed to benefit from security patches and bug fixes.
rationale: Running the latest version ensures protection against known vulnerabilities.

title: Ensure Server Tokens are set to Prod
description: The ServerTokens directive controls the information Apache returns in the HTTP response header.
rationale: Reducing the information returned by Apache makes it harder for attackers to identify the server version and determine applicable exploits.
